corrections.description: File contains content from 'Be a Professional Homemaker' article instead of Church Magazine Conversion Rules
corrections.text:
Church Magazine Conversion Rules
The following are rules the Church Magazine Office is using to apply The Era,
Instructor, and Relief Society credits toward the Adult Magazine.
The subscriber's name and address as it appears on The Era file will be used
for the Adult Magazine. The Era file is being used as the base for the Adult file.
The months remaining on an Instructor subscription and a Relief Society Magazine
subscription will be added to The Era subscription.
The number of Adult Magazines a subscriber receives will be determined by
the number of Eras being received. If a person is not receiving an Era, the number
of Adult Magazines he receives will be determined by the number of Instructors
being received. If a person is not receiving The Era or Instructor, the number of
Adult Magazines he receives will be determined by the number of Relief Society
Magazines being received. In the case of institutional subscribers such as seminaries,
institutes, and ward libraries, the number of Adult Magazines they will receive
will be determined by the largest number they are receiving of either The Era or
The Instructor.
Samples of typical situations encountered in the combining of Era and Relief
Society subscriptions are printed on the following pages. The rule being followed
in each case is stipulated. While it is realized on occasion these may not be the
actions subscribers desire, rules have to be adopted that would seem to apply in most
cases. Where a particular example fits your situation and the action taken isn't what
is desired, write and tell the Church Magazine Office at 79 South State Street, Salt
Lake City, Utah 84111, and they will attempt to follow your wishes.
1. If the complete name and address are exactly the same on the Relief Society Magazine
and Era list, the remaining months of the Relief Society Magazine beyond
December will be added to The Era.
SAMPLE
Era Subscription List Relief Society Magazine Subscription List
84302HUME-216E017 0CT71
OLIVE A HUME
216 E 3RD S
BRIGHAM CITY UT 84302
722
MRS. OLIVE A HUME
216 E 3RD S
BRIGHAM CITY UTAH 84302 JAN72
BRIG C-B C 17

corrections.description: File starts mid-sentence without proper beginning
corrections.text:
Be a PROFESSIONAL Homemaker, My Daughter
Lillian Y. Bradshaw

Taking you back to school the other night, sweet daughter nearly twenty, we talked of school and working and future plans of marriage. You said, "But, Mom, I lke to work. I like the excitement of it." I heard the echo of my own voice just that many years ago as I said to your father, "But honey, I want the challenge working outside the house would give me. I would feel like I'm doing something. I want to be someone, not just lost in diapers and dishes and scrubbing floors." And then I had bitterly added, "Anyone can do that!" I felt again a warm breeze blow a wisp of hair across my face as your father left for work that morning. Diaper and another diaper and another diaper blew in the Hawaiian breeze and tears streamed down my cheek. Thumb tackedthat's just how I feltthumb tacked to my home! Everytime I wanted to go out I had to weigh the cost of baby sitting, if I could find a baby sitter, against the food budget or think of little hands tugging at my skirts and crying to go home while I shopped just for the necessities. Resentment, acrid and hurting, choked all the beauty of my island home from my heart and mind and I longed to be free to go to work again. I had known the important feeling of being a head dietitian at one of the hospitals before I was married. I knew the feel of money of my own jingling in my pocketsto be free like my husband, talking with new vital people everyday. Just the luxury of talking real talk would seem like heaven and having luncheons. Which didn't have to be accounted for out of the family food budget! Like the diapers whipping vigorously, the harder my mind wrestled and twisted and thrashed as thoughts breezed through, the more I felt helplessly pinned. It was the treadmill of everyday blending into the last and so it seemed it would be forever! Emotionally spent and hurting all over with "outside-itis," out of the clear bluetoo audibly to denycame the thought quiet and pleading, '""Have you forgotten? Have you forgotten so soon?" Somersaulting one upon the other came thoughts of the prayers I once asked to find just the right man for me, then the prayer for a temple marriage, and then prayers that followed later that a healthy child, then another and another be born. Then sobbing in humility for all I had forgotten, I determined from that momentif I could not go out to find the business worldI would make our home my business and bring all I had learned into my home. I would find the excitement there. And if I couldn't find it I would create it. The same thought would go into running our home eflficiently as if I were still in the business world. I realized for the first time that my home held all the possibilities of achievement I could hope to find outside in any field.

corrections.description: File contains Relief Society Song Contest content instead of poem
corrections.text:
The Maturity of Seed
Margaret B. Shomaker

Yes, the maturity
Of seed
Glories with ripeness
With truththe Poet's goal

corrections.description: File contains Relief Society Song Contest content instead of poem
corrections.text:
Why Poetry?
Margaret B. Jorgensen

The flame of Phlox
And the molten color
Of the Virginia creeper
Brighten my cottage door
Enough to speak
Our thought
Or read another's as our own?
To soar with music,
Leap in dance,
Weep with prose,
Exult in drama?
Why, then the poet?
His to magnify a sunbeam
Till its focus burns into a soul,
Setting it afire
With truththe Poet's goal

corrections.description: File contains Relief Society Song Contest content instead of poem
corrections.text: OCR gap - poem text not clearly visible in source

corrections.description: File contains content from Editorial article at the end
corrections.text: Virginia Tanner, Utah's instructor of dancing, has been internationally acclaimed for her beautiful programs presented with creative children. She will now direct a pilot children's dance program sponsored by the National Endowments of the Arts. It will be conducted in four-week seminars in five United States school districts: in Pennsylvania, Alabama, Ohio, Oregon, and California. June Arey is director of dance in the National Endowments of the Arts.

corrections.description: File starts mid-recipe without proper beginning
corrections.text:
Cooking With Peanut Butter
Julia Read Clark

PEANUT BUTTER PIE
Serve 8 Serve 100
eggs 2 2 doz.
Corn syrup lec. 3 qt.
Sugar lec. 3 at.
Peanut butter YC, 6 c.
Vanilla 1 tsp. 2 tbsp.
Unbaked 8-inch pie crust 1 12 or 13
Beat eggs. Mix eggs, corn syrup, sugar, peanut butter, and vanilla together. Pour into unbaked pie crust. Bake at 350° (moderate oven) 45 to 60 minutes, until top is brown and crisp. This tastes like an old-fashioned molasses pie.

PEANUT BUTTER COOKIES
Yield 4 to 5 Dozen Yield 200
1 c. Fat 4c.
1 c. Peanut butter 4c.
2 c. Sugar, one white and one brown 4c.
2 eggs 8
22 c. Flour 3 qts.
14 tsp. Salt 1 tbsp.
Tsp. Soda 2 tsp.
Mix fat a

corrections.description: File contains content from Late Word poem
corrections.text:
OCTOBER 2ist
Eyes blue, tears of pain,
hair none, years come,
autumn day, years wane.
Golden sun. Loved and fed
Crisp air, then wed.
Leaves falling, It's nice to ''be.''
mother sewing, Thanks, Mom
daughter growing. For having me.
Laughing joy, Marilyn M. Oswald

corrections.description: File missing author name at end
File contains content from The Secret of Blueberry Hill
corrections.text:
HOPE
Snow, deep snow everywhere,
But high on a limb, a robin's there.
Grayce E. Cutler

corrections.description: File contains fiction content instead of being a poem file
corrections.text: This appears to be misnamed - contains fiction content from The Secret of Blueberry Hill, not a poem

notes: Several files have content bleeding issues, particularly poems that contain content from other articles. The OCR quality makes it difficult to extract clean poem text in some cases. The Church Magazine Conversion Rules article appears to have been completely replaced with content from another article.
